response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of the GISCollective geo-data platform, GIS apps for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and has built low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his C++ experience is brief and dated (roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, plus a thesis project); his systems work was in DLang, and recent years have centered on TypeScript/Node product engineering. He has no direct navigation or routing pipeline experience, so he is a stretch fit worth a screen only if the team can accept a C++ ramp-up.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS domain experience, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform
Proven low-latency distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and broad full-stack versatility
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated; no Rust experience listed
No direct navigation guidance, routing, or map-tile pipeline background
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node product work, several years removed from systems-level programming
